articles.title: Skill package structure
articles.summary: Canonical folders and files accepted by the local builder.
articles.body:
# Skill package structure

A v1 package has one top-level folder when exported. Inside the builder, paths are stored relative to that folder.

Expected files include `SKILL.md`, optional `README.md`, optional `LICENSE`, optional `metadata.json`, and optional folders `references/`, `scripts/`, `assets/`, `examples/`, and `evals/`.

articles.title: SKILL.md frontmatter
articles.summary: Required YAML fields for a valid skill entrypoint.
articles.body:
# SKILL.md frontmatter

Every skill needs YAML frontmatter at the top of `SKILL.md`.

```
---
name: csv-analysis-reporter
description: Analyze CSV files and generate markdown reports. Use when the user needs metrics, trends, or anomalies from tabular data.
---
```

Use simple `key: value` lines in v1.

articles.title: Naming rules
articles.summary: How to choose a compatible skill name.
articles.body:
# Naming rules

Use lowercase hyphen-separated words. Avoid spaces, underscores, uppercase letters, and generic names.

Good: `csv-analysis-reporter`

Bad: `CSV Analyzer`, `csv_analyzer`, `skill-helper`.

articles.title: Description writing
articles.summary: Write descriptions that help agents decide when to activate a skill.
articles.body:
# Description writing

A description should say what the skill does and when to use it. Include concrete task contexts.

Good: `Analyze CSV files and generate structured markdown reports. Use when the agent needs to summarize tabular metrics, trends, and anomalies.`

Avoid vague descriptions like `helps with tasks`.

articles.title: References, scripts, and assets
articles.summary: When to put content in supporting folders.
articles.body:
# References, scripts, and assets

Use `references/` for detailed domain rules, `scripts/` for deterministic helper code, and `assets/` for templates or media. If `SKILL.md` mentions `references/schema.md`, the file should exist in the package.

articles.title: Validation errors
articles.summary: How to read local validation reports.
articles.body:
# Validation errors

Errors block local approval and verified export. Warnings allow export but require review. Info messages are improvement hints.

The most important rule is exactly one root-level `SKILL.md`.

articles.title: Safe script handling
articles.summary: How v1 treats package scripts and suspicious content.
articles.body:
# Safe script handling

AI Skill Hub v1 never executes imported scripts. Scripts and hidden files are flagged for manual review. Remove secrets, private keys, destructive commands, and policy-bypass instructions before sharing a package.

articles.title: ZIP import troubleshooting
articles.summary: Common reasons a ZIP import is rejected.
articles.body:
# ZIP import troubleshooting

A valid archive contains one top-level folder and no absolute paths, parent-directory paths, duplicate normalized paths, nested archive surprises, or excessive file counts.

articles.title: Export for agent clients
articles.summary: How exported ZIP files are shaped.
articles.body:
# Export for agent clients

Export creates `<skill-name>-draft.zip` or `<skill-name>-<version>.zip`. Inside the archive is one top-level folder containing `SKILL.md` and supporting files.

articles.title: Local workspace backup
articles.summary: Move local drafts between browsers or machines.
articles.body:
# Local workspace backup

Workspace data is stored in IndexedDB in the current browser. Use Workspace -> Export backup to save a JSON backup, and Import backup to restore it elsewhere.
